description: Admin Console Transformation: every admin section (Dashboard, Live Map, Users, Companies, Drones, Pricing, Revenue, Compliance, Settings, Logs) currently looks stone-age - content strangled into narrow columns on a 1200px shell, emoji stat chips, no page headers, no grid composition, text-only loading. Transform it into a modern, space-efficient, beautiful dashboard. Full plan + audit: tasks/prd-admin-dashboard-transform.md. HARD CONSTRAINTS for every story: (1) never rename/remove element ids; (2) never rename CSS classes referenced by js/app/*.js - grep before touching; (3) never change inline onclick/onkeydown wiring; (4) never edit js/app.bundle.js - edit js/app/*.js then run 'npm run build:js'; (5) bump the ?v= cache-buster on BOTH the css/app.css link and the app.bundle.js script in app.html whenever either changes; (6) no smart/curly quotes in JS; (7) SHARED-CLASS TRAP: .pd-stat, .pd-loading, .pd-breakdown, .profile-stats-grid, .op-section-title, .op-empty, .op-btn, .dashboard-pill, .op-status-badge are also used by the passenger profile modal, passenger drone panel and operator console - create NEW adm-* classes or scope selectors under .admin-main; never restyle a shared class globally; (8) this environment is HEADLESS: no browser, no screenshots - verify every story with a static grep audit and write the evidence into progress.txt (US-019 precedent); do NOT claim visual verification; (9) scope is the admin console only (#admin-view in app.html, admin CSS in css/app.css, renderers in js/app/03-admin-profile.js and js/app/09-drones.js) - do not touch marketing pages, css/tokens.css, or passenger/operator views.

userStories.title: Admin shell space foundation: fluid width, 12-col grid system, page headers everywhere
userStories.description: As an admin on a modern monitor, I want the console to use my screen instead of squeezing everything into a ~950px column with 640px cards, and I want every section to open with a real page header so I always know where I am.
acceptanceCriteria: Fluid shell: .admin-shell max-width goes 1200px -> 1680px with padding clamp(16px, 3vw, 40px); update the @media (min-width:1600px) override at css/app.css ~5022 (currently 1280px) to match; drawer column stays 240px sticky; .admin-main keeps min-width:0

Kill the straitjacket: grep css/app.css and app.html for admin-section-card / admin-section-card--narrow usage (Pricing, Revenue, Compliance, Settings), then remove the 640px/560px max-width behaviour in favour of grid placement (keep the class names existing markup references, just change what they do; per-story layouts land in later stories)

New grid utilities in css/app.css: .adm-grid { display:grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); gap: 20px } plus .adm-span-3/-4/-6/-8/-12 column-span helpers; at <=1200px spans of 3/4 become 6, at <=768px everything becomes 12 (single column)

Consistent page headers: every admin section (users, live, add, companies, pricing, revenue, compliance, settings, drones, logs) opens with the same header pattern as the dashboard - a container using .admin-page-header with a 22-24px/700 navy title, a muted subtitle line (reuse the existing hint copy from the current .admin-meta-hint paragraphs), and a right-aligned actions slot; move the existing 'Add member' button into the Users header actions slot; all existing ids, data-admin-section wiring, and showAdminSection() calls unchanged

The lone .op-section-title eyebrows that used to open each section are replaced by (or wrapped in) the new header markup - but the .op-section-title CLASS itself is not restyled (shared with operator console); interior sub-headings may keep using it
Fix the broken markup at app.html ~line 1017: <div id="admin-users-company-filter" class="admin-company-filter" hidden is missing its closing '>' - repair it without renaming the id/class

#admin-summary-bar (app.html ~998) is dead markup no JS fills (0 hits in the bundle) - keep the element and its id but hide it via CSS for now (US-102 repurposes or finalizes it); note this decision in progress.txt

Grep evidence written to progress.txt: (a) new .admin-shell rule quoted, (b) .adm-grid rule quoted, (c) one grep line per admin section proving its header markup exists, (d) the repaired line 1017, (e) confirmation that no shared class listed in the PRD description gained new global rules
npm run build:js if any JS changed; bump ?v= cache-busters in app.html; npm run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All criteria met by prior stories (US-008/009/019/020). Grep evidence in progress.txt 2026-07-06 entry.

userStories.title: Dashboard command center: real KPI cards, composed grid, skeleton loading
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the Dashboard to read like a modern command center - big scannable KPI cards with icons and hierarchy, composed on a grid - instead of a flat wall of emoji chips borrowed from the passenger profile modal.
acceptanceCriteria: New admin-only stats renderer: loadAdminPlatformStats() in js/app/03-admin-profile.js stops injecting statsDashboardHtml() and instead renders new admin markup via a new function (e.g. adminDashboardHtml(stats)) consuming the SAME /api/me/stats response; the profile modal keeps using statsDashboardHtml() untouched

New .adm-kpi card CSS: white card, var(--radius-lg), var(--shadow-sm), hover var(--shadow-md); anatomy = 36-40px rounded icon chip with inline SVG stroke icon (NO emoji) on a soft tinted bg, 11px uppercase letter-spaced gray label, 28-32px/800 near-black value with tabular-nums, optional small context chip (e.g. live count in amber, completed in green)

KPI hierarchy on the .adm-grid: primary row of 4 .adm-span-3 cards (Total users, Total bookings, Live flights, Revenue); secondary row of smaller .adm-span-3/-4 tiles for Completed, Cancelled, CO2 saved, Distance flown, Aircraft available (compact variant, smaller value)

Composition row below KPIs: 'Users by role' card (.adm-span-6, restyled horizontal progress bars from the existing t.users breakdown data - new adm-* bar classes, not .pd-breakdown) and a 'Live operations' card (.adm-span-6) showing the live-flights number prominently with a 'Open Live Map' button wired to the existing showAdminSection('live')
#admin-summary-bar is either repurposed as a compact dateline/quick-context strip rendered by the new code or remains hidden - decide, implement, and record in progress.txt (id must survive)

Skeleton loading: new .adm-skeleton shimmer blocks (CSS keyframes animation, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion) shown by loadAdminPlatformStats() while fetching, replacing the 'Fetching platform stats...' text for this section; error state keeps a friendly retry message

No emoji characters remain in the admin dashboard markup (grep the new renderer output for emoji escapes); passenger profile modal and operator dashboards still use the old renderer untouched (grep proves statsDashboardHtml call sites)
Grep evidence in progress.txt: new renderer function + host injection line, .adm-kpi CSS rule, both call sites of statsDashboardHtml() proving profile modal untouched, skeleton CSS rule
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Users section as a modern data table with toolbar
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the Users section to feel like a proper data table - aligned columns, a clean toolbar, compact actions, a modern pager - instead of chunky stacked cards with four loud buttons each.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar row under the page header: the existing #admin-tab-customer/#admin-tab-operator buttons restyled as a segmented pill control (new admin-scoped CSS on the existing .admin-user-tabs/.admin-tab classes - grep first to confirm they are admin-only), the company filter select styled to match (44px, rounded, subtle border), and the meta count right-aligned as muted 13px text

Aligned rows: .admin-user-card rows (renderAdminUsers in js/app/03-admin-profile.js ~945) become grid rows with fixed column template (avatar+identity | role chip | company chip | status | actions | chevron) so every row lines up; 40px gradient avatar; name 14px/600 with email 12.5px muted beneath; divider-only separation, row hover var(--gray-50); whole-row click/keyboard wiring to openAdminUserDrawer unchanged

Actions become compact: the four .admin-btn-sm buttons restyle as small ghost/icon-labelled buttons (28-32px height) that are visually quiet until row hover (always visible on touch/mobile via media query); Delete keeps a danger treatment; all onclick handlers unchanged

Modern pager: renderAdminUsers pager area restyles as 'Showing 1-6 of N' muted text left + icon prev/next buttons right (chevron SVGs, disabled states); the 'loads 6 at a time' phrasing and 'Load 6 more on this page' button get cleaner copy (e.g. 'Load more'); existing adminUsersPrevPage/adminUsersNextPage/loadAdminUsersChunk wiring unchanged
Empty states for users keep the existing op-empty markup but gain an admin-scoped polish (centered, muted, soft icon tile) via .admin-main scoping - not by restyling .op-empty globally
Skeleton rows (3-4 .adm-skeleton table-row shapes) replace 'Fetching users...' while loading
Company filter select works with the markup repaired in US-101 (verify the hidden attribute toggling in JS still matches)

Grep evidence in progress.txt: new row grid CSS, toolbar CSS, pager markup lines from the renderer, proof all onclick handlers survived (grep adminResetPassword/adminSendResetOtp/adminBanUser/adminDeleteUser/openAdminUserDrawer)
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live Map as the hero: tall map, overlay chips, side-by-side panels
userStories.description: As an admin watching operations, I want the Live Map to dominate the section - a tall immersive map with floating status chips and the live/fleet lists beside it - not a 380px box with two stacked lists.
acceptanceCriteria: .admin-live-map height goes 380px -> clamp(420px, 62vh, 680px); border-radius var(--radius-xl); the fullscreen toggle button restyles as a floating white circular button with var(--shadow-md) (keep #admin-live-map-fs id and toggleAdminMapFullscreen wiring; verify the existing fullscreen mode still sizes correctly - grep the fullscreen CSS/JS)

#admin-live-meta renders as floating rounded chips overlaid on the map's top-left (white bg, var(--shadow-md), backdrop-filter blur with solid fallback) showing the counts the JS already writes - grep loadAdminLive (or equivalent) in js/app/03-admin-profile.js / 05-map.js for what fills it and wrap that output in chip markup

.admin-live-layout becomes a 2-col grid on desktop (the two .admin-live-panel cards side by side, collapsing to 1-col at <=900px); panels become white cards with the section sub-heading style, their own scroll areas (max-height ~340px, thin scrollbar), and divider-only rows

Live list rows: status dot/chip (in-transit amber, dispatching blue - map from the classes the renderer outputs), pilot name 14px/600, route/meta 12.5px muted, right-aligned time/coords in tabular-nums; fleet rows show a small gradient avatar + GPS freshness hint
Empty and loading states: skeleton rows while fetching; empty copy stays purposeful ('No live flights right now - dispatches will appear here')
5-second refresh behaviour untouched (do not touch the polling code beyond markup produced)
Grep evidence in progress.txt: new map height rule, overlay chip CSS + the renderer lines producing chips, 2-col .admin-live-layout rule, proof polling/refresh functions unchanged
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Companies partner directory and Add Member form polish
userStories.description: As an admin, I want Companies to read like a partner-account directory with rich cards, and Add Member to feel like a well-designed form, both composed on the new grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Company cards (grep loadAdminCompanies in js/app/03-admin-profile.js ~581 and the .partner-grid CSS for current anatomy): each card gets a consistent anatomy - 44px monogram avatar (code letters on navy-to-blue gradient), company name 15px/700, code chip, a counts row of icon+number pairs (pilots, offices, rating stars when present), hover lift (translateY(-2px) + var(--shadow-md)); expanded offices render as an indented sub-list with city/address/contact rows and divider separation; expand/collapse wiring unchanged
.partner-grid becomes responsive auto-fill columns (repeat(auto-fill, minmax(300px, 1fr)), gap 16-20px) so cards use the full main width

Add Company form card: header row ('Add company' sub-heading + helper text), the two .pd-input fields and button aligned in one row on desktop (stack at <=768px), inputs 44px rounded with focus ring; addOperatorCompany() wiring unchanged; note: .pd-input is shared - style via a scoped selector or an added class in the admin markup, never globally

Add Member section: the .admin-form-card composes as a max ~760px card on the grid with a 2-col field grid (grep the existing .admin-form-grid rule and upgrade it), labels 13px/500 var(--gray-600), inputs/selects 44-48px rounded with focus ring, the company/office selects appearing per the existing toggleAddMemberCompany() logic; the temp-password note becomes a soft info callout (light blue bg, rounded, small icon); primary CTA right-aligned in a card footer row; doAddUser wiring and all ids unchanged
Success/error feedback (#admin-add-error, #admin-add-success) styled as soft rounded alert chips consistent with the auth cards

Grep evidence in progress.txt: partner grid rule, card anatomy classes in the renderer output, form grid rule, proof of unchanged wiring (addOperatorCompany, doAddUser, toggleAddMemberCompany, onAddMemberCompanyChange)
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pricing and Settings: grouped forms that use the width
userStories.description: As an admin, I want Pricing to present its numbers as a grouped, confident configuration surface with its changelog beside it, and Settings to show its toggles as clean cards - no more 560px noodle columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Pricing layout: on desktop the section becomes a 2-col composition on .adm-grid - the pricing form card .adm-span-7/8 and the changelog .adm-span-5/4 beside it (stacking at <=1024px); grep renderPricingForm (js/app/03-admin-profile.js ~1298) for the field markup it generates before styling

Pricing form: fields grouped under small uppercase group headings (e.g. Surcharges / Taxes / Commission - derive groups from the actual fields the renderer outputs), inputs 44px rounded with a '%' suffix adornment (wrap inputs in a suffix container in the renderer), 2-3 fields per row on desktop; Save button is the single primary CTA in a card footer with the last-saved hint; save wiring unchanged
Changelog (renderPricingChangelog ~1344): restyled as a vertical timeline card - dot + line rail, each entry with who/when muted 12px and what 13px, newest first; empty state friendly

Settings: the two toggle cards (#admin-toggle-emergency-bypass, #admin-toggle-demo-mode) become .adm-span-6 cards side by side on desktop; each card = icon chip + 15px/600 title + 13px muted description + the toggle switch top-right; the .admin-toggle-track/.admin-toggle-knob switch restyles to the modern 48x28 sliding-knob language (green when on); all checkbox ids and onchange handlers unchanged
Toggle status lines (#admin-bypass-status, #admin-demo-status) render as small soft chips (green enabled / gray disabled) instead of plain text
Skeletons for pricing fetch replace 'Fetching pricing configuration...' text

Grep evidence in progress.txt: 2-col pricing composition, group-heading + suffix markup from the renderer, timeline CSS, settings card grid, toggle CSS, proof toggleEmergencyBypass/toggleDemoMode wiring unchanged
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Revenue: KPI row, modern chart, confident payouts table
userStories.description: As an admin, I want Revenue to look like a finance dashboard - KPI cards, a readable 30-day chart, and a crisp payouts table - not emoji chips over a wall of cramped bars.
acceptanceCriteria: renderRevenueKPIs (js/app/03-admin-profile.js ~1378) switches from pdStat() to the .adm-kpi card language from US-102 (4 cards on the grid: Total Revenue, This Month, Platform Commission, Completed Trips) with SVG icons, no emoji; #admin-revenue-kpis keeps its id (drop the now-inappropriate profile-stats-grid class from that container in app.html only if no JS references it - grep first, otherwise scope styles)

Chart card (renderRevenueChart ~1388): full-width white card with header row (title + total-for-period muted value right); bars become rounded-top gradient columns (blue -> sky) with hover highlight, min-height 240px plot area, horizontal gridlines via CSS background, y-max label top-left, and x-axis labels decimated via CSS (show every ~5th label with :nth-child, all still in title tooltips); empty state keeps friendly copy in the new card language

Payouts table (renderRevenuePayouts ~1407): wrapped in a white card with header row; modern table language - 11-12px uppercase gray header, divider-only rows, row hover, numeric columns right-aligned with tabular-nums, pilot name with small avatar monogram, net payout column emphasized (semibold navy), commission muted; horizontal scroll wrapper preserved for mobile
Section composes on .adm-grid: KPIs row, then chart .adm-span-12, payouts .adm-span-12 (or -8 with a top-payouts summary side card if data supports it - keep to existing API fields only)
Skeleton KPI cards + a skeleton chart block during fetch
Grep evidence in progress.txt: new renderer output lines (no pdStat call remains in revenue functions), chart CSS rules, table CSS rules, proof loadAdminRevenue endpoint/params untouched
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Compliance status board and terminal-style Logs
userStories.description: As an admin, I want Compliance to read as a status board with clear severity zones, and Logs to feel like a purposeful terminal view instead of a bare text list.
acceptanceCriteria: Compliance KPIs: renderComplianceSummary (js/app/03-admin-profile.js ~1452) switches from pdStat() to .adm-kpi cards (Total Pilots, Checked In 24h green, Missing amber, Failed red) with SVG icons; container id kept

Status-board grid: 'Missing Checklist (24h)' and 'Failed Checklists (7 days)' cards sit side by side (.adm-span-6 each, stack <=1024px) with a colored left accent bar (amber/red), count chip in the card header, and divider-only rows; 'Recent Checklists' becomes a full-width card with table-like rows and PASS/FAIL chips right-aligned

Row anatomy in all three lists: pilot monogram avatar, name 14px/600, email/time muted 12.5px, chip right-aligned - upgrade the renderer markup (renderComplianceMissing/Failed/Recent) with new adm-* classes

Logs: #admin-logs-scroll becomes a dark terminal card (deep navy #0B1F3A-family bg, var(--radius-lg)), 65vh height, monospace 12.5px, thin custom scrollbar; grep the logs renderer in js/app for the row/level markup it outputs and colorize levels as small chips or colored level tokens (error red / warn amber / info gray-blue); timestamps muted; scroll-up lazy-load behaviour untouched
Logs header row: #admin-logs-meta styles as a sticky meta bar above the terminal (count + hint) with a monospace-feel; 'Fetching older logs...' indicator restyled to fit the dark surface
Skeletons for the compliance fetch; logs keep their incremental loader

Grep evidence in progress.txt: no pdStat call remains in compliance functions, accent-card CSS, terminal CSS rule, logs renderer lines with level colorization, proof lazy-load scroll handler unchanged
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drones admin: segmented tabs, service card grid, consistent forms
userStories.description: As an admin, I want Drone Management to match the rest of the transformed console - segmented sub-tabs, a scannable services grid, and clean lists - instead of loose pills and raw lists.
acceptanceCriteria: The three sub-tab buttons (#drone-admin-tab-services/-operators/-bookings) currently use the SHARED .dashboard-pill class (also used by the passenger drone panel in js/app/09-drones.js:35-37) - add a new class in the admin markup (e.g. adm-seg-tab) alongside the existing one and style THAT as a segmented control matching US-103's Users tabs; showDroneAdminTab wiring unchanged; passenger drone pills visually unchanged (grep proof)

Services list (grep the renderer for #drone-admin-services-list in js/app/09-drones.js): cards on a responsive auto-fill grid - service name 14px/700, category chip, price emphasized right, status chip (active/inactive), compact edit/action buttons; existing handlers unchanged

Operators and Bookings sub-tabs: rows upgraded to the aligned divider-only row language (avatar/monogram, name+meta stack, status chip, right-aligned values); bookings stats strip (#drone-admin-bookings-stats) uses compact .adm-kpi tiles

Add Service / Add Operator forms (showDroneServiceForm/showDroneOperatorForm renderers): form fields adopt the 44px rounded + focus-ring language via new adm-* or scoped classes; single primary CTA; cancel as ghost button; wiring unchanged
Sub-header rows (.drone-sub-header) align sub-heading left + action button right, action button styled like the Users header action
Skeletons replace the three 'Fetching drone ...' loading texts within the admin drones section only

Grep evidence in progress.txt: segmented tab markup with both classes present, passenger pill renderer untouched (quote js/app/09-drones.js:35-37), services grid CSS, form field classes, proof all onclick handlers survived
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes

userStories.title: Polish sweep and static audit evidence (gate)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the whole console to feel cohesive and alive - consistent motion, hover states, spacing rhythm, responsive behaviour - and as the team, we want machine-checkable evidence that the transformation is complete and regression-free.
acceptanceCriteria: Section-switch motion: showAdminSection() target sections get a subtle fade+4px-rise entrance (CSS animation ~180ms on .admin-section when shown), fully disabled under prefers-reduced-motion; wiring unchanged

Interactive-state sweep across ALL new adm-* elements: every button/card/row/tab/chip added in US-101..US-109 has hover, focus-visible (var(--focus-ring)), and where relevant active/disabled states - fix stragglers found by grepping the new classes against css/app.css

Skeleton coverage: every admin section fetch shows .adm-skeleton shapes (no bare 'Fetching ...' text remains inside #admin-view sections - grep app.html and the admin renderers; the passenger/operator .pd-loading usages stay untouched)
Spacing rhythm audit: section padding, card padding (20-24px), grid gaps (16-20px), and header margins follow a 4px base - normalize offenders in the admin CSS block

Responsive audit by CSS review (no browser): verify and fix rules so the admin shell has no fixed widths that overflow at 360/768/1024/1440/1920 - drawer collapses per existing toggleAdminDrawer behaviour at <=768px, grids collapse per US-101, map/table wrappers scroll internally; document the checked breakpoints

Write tasks/admin-transform-audit.md: one row per admin section [section | page header present (quote line) | grid composition used (classes) | skeleton loading (yes/renderer line) | remaining inline style= count (must be 0 for admin markup) | hardcoded hex in its new CSS (must be 0, tokens only) | shared classes untouched (grep proof)] plus a final constraint checklist (ids intact, bundle rebuilt, ?v= bumped, typecheck passing)

MANDATORY gate: only set passes:true when tasks/admin-transform-audit.md exists AND every row shows header=yes, skeletons=yes, 0 inline styles, 0 hardcoded hex in new CSS; record the audit path in notes; do NOT claim visual/browser verification
npm run build:js; bump ?v= cache-busters; npm run typecheck passes
